what_it_does: Styles a 'Source' detail surface: a fixed-position overlay card (plus scrim) that shows the expanded record behind a tapped dot, including a close button, verb tag, name, meaning, body label, formatted body text, operator notes, empty/status messages, and a provenance footer. It is anchored bottom-right on wide viewports and becomes a bottom sheet on narrow ones.
observations: Pure CSS; no JavaScript symbols or selectors.
Uses CSS custom properties for color, spacing, typography, radius, and shadow tokens.
Scrim and card are fixed-positioned with explicit z-index values 58 and 59.
Includes two entrance animations: src-fade for the scrim and src-pop for the card.
Responsive breakpoint at max-width: 560px converts the card to a full-width bottom sheet.
Body text preserves whitespace and line breaks with white-space: pre-wrap.
Operator notes are styled as a separate list of quiet cards with a different background token.
Close button has a focus-visible outline style.
Comments explain the intended layering relative to the V handle and gallery modal.
standouts: Extensive opening comment that documents the design intent, z-index contract, and token philosophy.
Strict avoidance of hardcoded colors; everything is var()-based.
Distinct visual treatment for system content versus the user's own notes.
Responsive behavior switches anchor from corner panel to thumb-reachable bottom sheet.
conventions: Token-driven theming via CSS custom properties.
Animation keyframes prefixed to match the component.
Desktop-first responsive rule, then narrow override.
Focus-visible for keyboard accessibility on the close control.
concerns: Some hardcoded pixel values mixed with tokens, e.g., 56px handle clearance, 28px close button, 160ms animation duration.
z-index 58/59 are file-local and not obviously centralized; may be brittle if other surfaces change.
No prefers-reduced-motion guard for the entrance animations.
Default box-sizing means padding and border add to the stated max-height, which could slightly exceed intended viewport bounds.
The 'Source verb', 'V', and note store are referenced in comments but not defined in this file.
notes: A token-driven stylesheet for a source-detail overlay, with clear separation between system record and operator notes and a deliberate responsive bottom-sheet behavior.
questions: What is the 'V' and the 'Source verb' in the broader application?
Where are the referenced tokens such as --ground-primary and --ink-faint defined?
Is the 56px V handle height also tokenized elsewhere?
How are operator notes populated from the store mentioned in the comments?
Is there a global z-index registry that owns the 58/59 values?
apparent_themes: Explicit layered UI with z-index contracts
Distinction between system content and personal notes
intention_signals: To present a polished, consistent reading card that surfaces provenance and meaning without overwhelming the user, while staying visually harmonious with adjacent surfaces and respecting safe areas.
novelty: The 'Source verb' concept and the detailed narrative comments about z-index layering relative to the V handle and gallery modal; the explicit visual separation of system body content from operator notes.
contribution: Provides the visual shell, responsive layout, animations, focus states, content formatting, and status/empty-state styling for a source-detail overlay, plus a distinct notes-list style that a fused system would keep if it uses this component.
summary_for_embedding: CSS stylesheet defining the layout, appearance, animations, and responsive bottom-sheet behavior of a 'Source' detail card overlay, including its scrim, close control, record body, operator notes, and status states.
intention_for_embedding: To render a readable, token-driven source-information panel that stays visually consistent with sibling surfaces and adapts from a corner-anchored card on wide viewports to a thumb-reachable bottom sheet on narrow ones.
